item.title: The save act failed
item.description: There was a lot of misinformation surrounding this, Republicans were trying to frame it as you need to just simply show ID to vote, and it was not just that, it was forcing people to get a passport in the United States and then the passport needing to have the last name match the birth certificate, which would’ve disenfranchise millions of women and people who may have changed their names in life, it’s never been about a simple ID. Most people already support a simple ID. The only way Republicans win is by prevent preventing people from voting.
item.dc:creator: wafflesdidit

item.title: Phone Clearing Dump (Mostly Gaming MEMES, That I Pulled From My Vault To Help You Get Trough Friday) #819
item.description:
1. She went to bard's college and is ashamed of it? She doesn't like magic?!
2. WTF?!
3
4. Somewhere out there some dev spent two years of their life tirelessly working on game, for it to just in on your backlog, while you to play Animal Crossing for the 1000th time
5
6. I wish I had a copy of OOunga-Bunga 24 (20 2024 B.C. that is)
7. How do we know they are not NPCs
8. Wish we could bring back all of these!
9. You ruined gaming!
10
11. Why Netflix, why?!?!?
12
13. Dang it! Just let me have fun!
14. Look at that cat's face!!!
Hello everyone! We are a two brother team and we are making a game inspired by the book Obedience to Authority as well as media such as Papers, Please and the show Severance. Here in Eastern Europe we have experience and past with harsh authority and authoritarianism. I mean look at our oppressive leftover architecture! I guess the subject has always interested me... "Why do good people follow evil orders?" Our game tests if you obey orders and how does your moral compass work (if at all). I invite you try it! Check out our Steam page and if you like the premise, please wishlist! ? https://store.steampowered.com/app/2719670/The_Milgram_Experiment Have a happy Friday!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.dc:creator: TinyOctopus

item.title: GovBrief Today #392
item.description: Eight days ago a federal judge fined a government lawyer $500 a day for defying her order. That was the warning shot. Today, Minnesota Chief Judge Patrick Schiltz threatened criminal contempt after his review found more than 200 court order violations since January. Another judge ruled the IRS broke the law 42,695 times by bulk-feeding confidential taxpayer addresses to ICE. Federal judges have stopped issuing warnings. They're building cases. And while courts count the crimes, the deportations keep coming. DHS admitted to Congress it deported 86 people protected under DACA, people who grew up here and passed federal background checks every time they renewed. Three senators are demanding answers. DHS Secretary Kristi Noem claimed most had "criminal histories" but refused to provide a single detail. Call your representatives and demand Noem's resignation or impeachment. The courts are doing their job. Congress needs to do theirs. We've emailed 6,300+ stories over 392 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/melania-un [2] https://go.govbrief.today/fda-bonus [3] https://go.govbrief.today/hclinton-depo [4] https://go.govbrief.today/epstein-cooperating-witnesses [5] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-sues-5-states [6] https://go.govbrief.today/anthropic-holds-fast [7] https://go.govbrief.today/border-drone-downed [8] https://go.govbrief.today/wyden-nsa [9] https://go.govbrief.today/minn-chief-judge [10] https://go.govbrief.today/irs-violated-law-ice [11] https://go.govbrief.today/fed-subpoena-fight [12] https://go.govbrief.today/ballroom-goldberg [13] https://go.govbrief.today/judge-ca-truckers [14] https://go.govbrief.today/abrego-garcia-motion [15] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-syria-tps [16] https://go.govbrief.today/daca-deportations [17] https://go.govbrief.today/mamdani-wh-feb [18] https://go.govbrief.today/ntsb-house-bill [19] https://go.govbrief.today/brady-tkachuk-video [20] https://go.govbrief.today/staffer-trump-memes [21] https://go.govbrief.today/denver-fed-agents Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: The fallacy of nonviolence
item.description: Kwame Ture in the documentary The Black Power Mictape. To those that engage in color-blind universalism and wish for peaceful protest: remind oneself how MLK jrs endeavors ended. Why is Mr. True referring to Doctor King in the past tense?

item.title: I’m shocked! Well, not that shocked
item.description: Most viral edit: Link to the study that contains the data for this article: https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/full/10.1161/JAHA.125.046517?mod=ANLink Original post: I’m in this article, but not dead, yet. I’ve skipped doctor appointments because I didn’t have money, or insurance. If only there was some sort of subsidized national healthcare system that incentivized people to get routine medical care before things get too serious and cost thousands of dollars to treat. (Note: same as every other healthcare post I make about the lack of universal healthcare in America; downvote, block me and move on. I’m not interested in Fox News talking points about universal healthcare and wait times to see a doctor in Denmark)
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.dc:creator: WolvesAreTheBestPeople

item.title: Cancelling elections - It Can Now Be Plainly Said: Trump Is Planning a November Coup d’État.
item.description:
Back in 2024, Kamala Harris and the Democrats struggled to convince voters that a second Donald Trump term would constitute a serious threat to democracy. Washington Post - His admin are circulating a 17-page draft executive order that claims China interfered in the 2020 election as a basis to declare a national emergency that would unlock extraordinary presidential power over voting. The plan would mandate voter ID and ban mail-in balloting, and calls on Trump to issue an executive order announcing both measures. https://newrepublic.com/post/207138/trump-coup-detat-midterm-elections
Their plan- in October Trump declares a state of emergency and postpones the election. The Supreme Court issues an emergency stay, saying he can’t do that. But the court has no army, and Trump does, along with a handful of lickspittle governors who just might follow him down whatever dark path he plows. That, not to mince words, is a coup d’état.
There will be no elections.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: The Republican's evil anti-trans hate legislation goes far beyond removing licenses.
item.description: https://time.com/7381312/transgender-kansas-id-bathroom-bill/ It essentially creates a bounty system for people to attack trans people, or anyone who they dont think looks femme enough, based on which bathrooms they use. Republicans are pedo-nazis. They're evil fucking monsters.

item.title: Mahogany seed pod
item.description: Mahogany seed pods are the 8–15 cm long, woody,, brown coverings from Swietenia mahagoni trees, often used as natural, long-lasting decoration in aquariums, terrariums, or floral arrangements. They are buoyant, release beneficial tannins for aquarium water, and crack open to release helicopter-like seeds. The helicopter seeds are visible on the ground
item.dc:creator: DOcelot1
